methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The total amount of payment tokens deducted from the buyer exactly equals the sum of tokens credited to the seller and the fee collector.
invariants.attack_scenario: Fee leakage or creation of tokens out of thin air during a purchase, leading to loss of funds for the buyer or protocol insolvency.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_payment_conservation() public view { assert(buyerBalanceBefore - buyerBalanceAfter == (sellerBalanceAfter - sellerBalanceBefore) + (feeCollectorBalanceAfter - feeCollectorBalanceBefore)); }

invariants.description: The vesting contract's token balance must always be greater than or equal to the total unclaimed vested tokens across all beneficiaries.
invariants.attack_scenario: Insolvency of the vesting contract, leaving users unable to claim their legitimately vested tokens.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_solvency() public view { assert(token.balanceOf(address(this)) >= totalUnclaimedTokens); }

invariants.description: When transferring vesting allocations between users, the sum of available (unclaimed) tokens across the grantor and beneficiary must remain constant.
invariants.attack_scenario: A transfer operation artificially inflates or deflates the total available tokens, leading to contract insolvency or loss of user funds.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_transfer_conservation() public view { assert(availableBefore == availableAfter); }

invariants.description: The claimable amount for any beneficiary must never exceed their total allocated amount minus the amount they have already claimed.
invariants.attack_scenario: A user claims more tokens than they were allocated, draining the vesting contract and causing insolvency for other users.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_claimable_limit() public view { (uint256 claimableAmount, ) = claimable(user); assert(claimableAmount <= _vestings[user].totalAmount - _vestings[user].amountClaimed); }

invariants.description: A user's total sold allocation cannot exceed their allowed sell limit, which is dynamically calculated based on their bought amount and the vesting plan's max sell percentage.
invariants.attack_scenario: A user bypasses the maximum sell percentage, dumping more tokens on the market than permitted and crashing the token price.
invariants.invariant_logic: userAllocation.sold <= userAllocation.bought + ((currentAlloc + userAllocation.sold - userAllocation.bought) * maxSellPercent) / BASE
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_max_sell_limit() public view { assert(allocations[user][plan].sold <= calculateSellLimit(user, plan)); }

invariants.description: The discounted price per unit must never exceed the original price per unit.
invariants.attack_scenario: A bug or underflow in the discount calculation causes the price to increase, unfairly overcharging the buyer.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_discount_price() public view { assert(_getDiscountedPrice(listing, amount) <= listing.pricePerUnit); }

invariants.description: The remaining balance of a marketplace listing can never exceed its initial total amount.
invariants.attack_scenario: A listing's balance is artificially inflated, allowing the seller to sell more tokens than they actually deposited into the marketplace.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_listing_balance() public view { assert(listings[plan][id].balance <= listings[plan][id].total); }
